active_question: In Anatomie Studio’s rope-hygiene guidance, what must all parties be able to give before deciding how rope will be used?
edited_answer: Because personal boundaries and risk profiles vary, so every party must be able to give informed consent.
edited_question: Why does Anatomie Studio say rope-hygiene choices must be discussed rather than assumed?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement explains why informed consent is necessary for rope-hygiene choices instead of training a bare two-word answer.
reason: The replacement explains why informed consent is necessary for rope-hygiene choices instead of training a bare two-word answer.
support_evidence: Whatever you decide, it’s important to make sure all parties are able to give informed consent. Personal boundaries and individual risk profiles are very different and just because you’re ok (or not) with something, doesn’t mean it will be the same for your partner(s).
